Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 78 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 170 upvotes, 23 comments
  3. Can I run AI locally? 1328 upvotes, 323 comments
  4. Michael Faraday: Scientist and Nonconformist (1996) 30 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 563 upvotes, 166 comments
  6. Python: The Optimization Ladder 114 upvotes, 27 comments
  7. Secure Secrets Management for Cursor Cloud Agents 30 upvotes, 4 comments
  8. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 390 upvotes, 120 comments
  9. Cookie jars capture American kitsch (2023) 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 145 upvotes, 42 comments
  11. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 986 upvotes, 388 comments
  12. Hammerspoon 321 upvotes, 116 comments
  13. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 46 upvotes, 11 comments
  14. Show HN: Ink – Deploy full-stack apps from AI agents via MCP or Skills 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 111 upvotes, 43 comments
  16. Philosoph Jürgen Habermas Gestorben 60 upvotes, 24 comments
  17. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 149 upvotes, 91 comments
  18. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 149 upvotes, 102 comments
  19. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 90 upvotes, 58 comments
  20. Nominal Types in WebAssembly 18 upvotes, 7 comments
  21. Recursive Problems Benefit from Recursive Solutions 39 upvotes, 21 comments
  22. Jürgen Habermas, influential German philosopher, dies at 96 9 upvotes, 2 comments
  23. XML Is a Cheap DSL 169 upvotes, 140 comments
  24. Starlink Militarization and Its Impact on Global Strategic Stability 52 upvotes, 37 comments
  25. Digg is gone again 306 upvotes, 316 comments
  26. Coding after coders: The end of computer programming as we know it? 178 upvotes, 274 comments
  27. Wired headphone sales are exploding 299 upvotes, 511 comments
  28. I beg you to follow Crocker's Rules, even if you will be rude to me 116 upvotes, 188 comments
  29. Parsing semiconductor datasheets into structured register maps for under $0.25 3 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. 9 Mothers Defense (YC P26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 12:30am, 15 March 2026. Source here.
